name: Algebraic Data Types
def: Composite types built from sums (variants/OR) and products (tuples/structs/AND).
problem: Model data so that only valid states are representable, with compiler-checked case handling.
mechanism: Sum types (tagged unions) enumerate alternatives; product types combine fields; pattern matching deconstructs them exhaustively.
limits: Adding a variant ripples through matches (often a feature)
Awkward to encode in C/older Java
failures: Boolean/flag soup instead of proper sums
whenNot: Open extensibility of variants needed (consider OOP/visitors)
myths: "ADT = abstract data type" — here it means algebraic data type
